summary: Revalidated ADR 0254 against the latest origin/main commit bae420263872e079fdc34f7f755a6984a3cd5949 and confirmed the branch implementation is correct but exact-main durability is still partial: Proxmox still exposes the 8096 host firewall lane, coolify-lv3 still serves ServerClaw locally and accepts bootstrap admin sign-in, yet current mainline truth still lacks the ServerClaw topology so the coolify guest firewall has drifted back to {80,443,8000} for nginx-lv3 and public https://chat.lv3.org/ currently redirects to https://nginx.lv3.org/ until merge-to-main and replay from merged main.

verification.observed: `git diff --stat origin/main -- inventory/group_vars/platform.yml inventory/host_vars/proxmox_florin.yml playbooks/serverclaw.yml docs/runbooks/configure-serverclaw.md collections/ansible_collections/lv3/platform/roles/linux_guest_firewall/templates/nftables.conf.j2 collections/ansible_collections/lv3/platform/roles/nginx_edge_publication/defaults/main.yml collections/ansible_collections/lv3/platform/roles/nginx_edge_publication/tasks/main.yml` shows current `origin/main` still lacks the ADR 0254 topology, playbook, runbook, guest-firewall, and public-edge publication surfaces that keep `chat.lv3.org` durable under server-resident reconciliation.

verification.check: Focused ADR 0254 validation and generated truth refresh
verification.observed: After regenerating the dependent catalogs and diagrams on top of current `origin/main`, `python3 scripts/validate_service_completeness.py --validate` passed and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/platform_manifest.py --check` passed, confirming the branch still carries internally consistent ADR 0254 truth after the latest merge refresh.

A fresh host probe on `proxmox_florin` still returned `PRETTY_NAME=Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie)`, kernel `6.17.13-2-pve`, `pve-manager/9.1.6/71482d1833ded40a (running kernel: 6.17.13-2-pve)`, and `/etc/pve/firewall/170.fw` line `17:IN ACCEPT -source 10.10.10.10/32 -p tcp -dport 8096`.

verification.check: Guest-local ServerClaw runtime and bootstrap auth
verification.observed: Fresh `coolify-lv3` checks still returned `WEBUI_URL=https://chat.lv3.org`, `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN=https://chat.lv3.org`, the expected OIDC, Ollama, and SearXNG endpoints in `/run/lv3-secrets/serverclaw/runtime.env`, a healthy `serverclaw` container on `0.0.0.0:8096->8080`, local `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` from `http://127.0.0.1:8096/`, and JSON for `ops@lv3.org` with `role":"admin"` plus a bearer token from `http://127.0.0.1:8096/api/v1/auths/signin`.

Fresh `coolify-lv3` nftables output now shows `ip saddr 10.10.10.10 tcp dport { 80, 443, 8000 } accept` without `8096`, while `nft` still carries a local DNAT rule for tcp/8096 and the app stays healthy. A fresh `nginx-lv3` probe therefore timed out on `10.10.10.70:8096`, proving the guest-firewall lane has drifted even though the host firewall and the app itself are still good.

Fresh public probes now return `HTTP/1.1 308 Permanent Redirect` on `http://chat.lv3.org/` and `HTTP/2 308` with `location: https://nginx.lv3.org/` on `https://chat.lv3.org/`; the public hostname is therefore still falling back to the generic edge redirect instead of the dedicated ServerClaw app.

The branch still carries the repo automation fixes uncovered during this workstream: `platform/policy/toolchain.py` now scopes cached OPA and Conftest binaries by platform, and `config/check-runner-manifest.json` plus `config/validation-gate.json` now give `yaml-lint` a `240` second timeout for local amd64-container replays on arm64 hosts.

notes: This receipt now records the latest exact-main candidate state before merge; it is intentionally not a final merged-main pass receipt yet.
The earlier branch-local receipt remains preserved as the first latest-main candidate replay that initially proved the dedicated ServerClaw surface.

Durable repair remains: merge ADR 0254 to main, cut the next patch release, replay `make converge-serverclaw` from merged main, then update shared release files plus `versions/stack.yaml` only after `chat.lv3.org` serves the app again.
Targeted coolify and nginx slice replays can temporarily restore the path before merge, but server-resident reconciliation following current main can still revert it until the topology merges.
